data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-03T00:54:27.038Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is a knowledge question about EF Core and the Cosmos DB provider. Let me answer based on what I know.

In EF Core, the Azure Cosmos DB provider has been moving toward async-only APIs. In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), synchronous operations on the Cosmos DB provider were deprecated or required an opt-in flag. The key change in .NET 11 (EF Core 11) would be the complete removal of sync-over-async support for the Cosmos DB provider.
 
Let me reconsider the timeline more carefully. EF Core 9 with .NET 9 is when the Cosmos provider actually dropped synchronous API support entirely, so any code calling sync LINQ operations would fail at that point rather than in the .NET 10 to .NET 11 transition.

For .NET 11 and EF Core 11, I should be cautious since that's a future version beyond my knowledge cutoff, but based on the trajectory of EF Core's direction toward async-only patterns, it would likely continue enforcing async-only behavior for the Cosmos provider with no workarounds available. and `SaveChangesAsync()` become the only viable options for database operations in .NET 11.
